jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic D'oh sound
jokes.explanation: Cultural touchstone catchphrase that became part of the Oxford Dictionary. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: That's all the time we have for today. I'd like to thank Sideshow Mel... Corporal Punishment, Tina Ballerina... and from Knots Landing, Miss Donna Mills. Oh, she was a sport.
jokes.setup: Standard talk show outro thanking guests
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous guest names including 'Corporal Punishment' mixed with real celebrity
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor mixing fake ridiculous names with real celebrity reference

jokes.text: You've got a merchandising meeting. - Cancel it! - Therapist? Cancel it! The line on the Giants is 5½. Put a dime on it.
jokes.setup: Assistant listing Krusty's appointments
jokes.punchline: Krusty cancels everything except gambling bet
jokes.explanation: Shows Krusty's priorities and character flaws through escalating cancellations

jokes.text: 'Thank you' dinner with Bart Simpson? I don't know Bart Simpson. The boy who saved you from jail. Well, we made a terrible, terrible mistake.
jokes.setup: Krusty claims not to know Bart Simpson
jokes.punchline: Admits it was a 'terrible mistake' to acknowledge the boy who saved him
jokes.explanation: Classic Krusty selfishness and ingratitude toward Bart

jokes.text: It's Miss Pennycandy, I can assure you.
jokes.setup: Marge addresses her as Mrs. Pennycandy
jokes.punchline: Bitter correction emphasizing her single status
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing bitterness about single status

jokes.text: How can he hurt someone who loves him so? Oh, Mrs. Simpson... I've wasted my womanhood asking that same question.
jokes.setup: Marge asks sympathetically about Krusty hurting Bart
jokes.punchline: Pennycandy reveals her own romantic pain with Krusty
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about wasted life and unrequited love, well-structured reveal

jokes.text: Lousy mildew!
jokes.setup: Claims something important came up preventing Krusty from dinner
jokes.punchline: The 'important' thing is dealing with mildew
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from 'important' to mundane household problem

jokes.text: How many Krusty autographs do you want? A hundred. Done.
jokes.setup: Apologizing for Krusty's absence
jokes.punchline: Immediately shifts to forging mass quantities of his signature
jokes.explanation: Shows the casual fraud and disrespect in Krusty's operation

jokes.text: I always suspected that nothing in life mattered. Now I know for sure. Get bent. Bart Simpson.
jokes.setup: Bart writing a disappointed letter to Krusty
jokes.punchline: Philosophical nihilism followed by childish 'get bent'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart voice mixing sophisticated disillusionment with juvenile language. Excellent character writing.

jokes.text: Ooh, SEX-CHAT!
jokes.setup: Homer sees advertisement
jokes.punchline: Excited reaction to sex chat line ad
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment, typical Homer reaction but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Hello? Are there any women here? Hello?! Are you beautiful? Do I sound beautiful?! This is not as hot as I had anticipated.
jokes.setup: Homer calls sex chat line expecting to talk to women
jokes.punchline: Awkward conversation with another confused male caller
jokes.explanation: Good escalating awkwardness as both callers realize their mistake

jokes.text: Hey! Not the face!
jokes.setup: Pennycandy threatening Krusty to make him go to dinner
jokes.punchline: Krusty's vain concern about his face during beating
jokes.explanation: Classic slapstick line showing Krusty's vanity even while being beaten

jokes.text: Bart, wipe your feet. Why bother? They'll just get dirty again.
jokes.setup: Marge asks Bart to wipe his feet
jokes.punchline: Bart's logical but unhelpful nihilistic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - technically correct but missing the point entirely

jokes.text: Milhouse, you can come in, and drop the charade.
jokes.setup: Everyone thinks it's Krusty at the door
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately recognizes it's Milhouse pretending
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Homer being unexpectedly perceptive

jokes.text: Go wait in the car.
jokes.setup: Krusty being told he can relax and be himself
jokes.punchline: Immediately dismisses Milhouse coldly
jokes.explanation: Shows Krusty's true dismissive personality when he's 'being himself'

jokes.text: Aw! We could have seen a monkey.
jokes.setup: Milhouse being dismissed from seeing Krusty
jokes.punchline: Disappointed he's missing what he thinks is a performing monkey
jokes.explanation: Child-like disappointment and misunderstanding of what Krusty is

jokes.text: Baruch ata Adonai... Eloheinu melech ha-olam... ha-motzi lechem min ha-aretz.
jokes.setup: Family expects typical TV host behavior
jokes.punchline: Krusty recites Hebrew prayer instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection revealing Krusty's hidden identity and setting up the episode's theme

jokes.text: A Jewish entertainer? Get out of here!
jokes.setup: Lisa explains Krusty must be Jewish
jokes.punchline: Homer's shocked disbelief at concept of Jewish entertainers
jokes.explanation: Good satire of ignorance about Jewish contributions to entertainment

jokes.text: Mel Brooks is Jewish?!
jokes.setup: Lisa lists prominent Jewish entertainers including Mel Brooks
jokes.punchline: Homer's continued shock about obvious examples
jokes.explanation: Continues Homer's obliviousness, escalating the ignorance

jokes.text: Poor Krusty. He's like a velvet painting come to life.
jokes.setup: Marge sympathizing with sad Krusty
jokes.punchline: Compares him to tacky velvet art
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly original and specific comparison that perfectly captures Krusty's tragic kitsch quality

jokes.text: Hey, Krusty, you going to finish that meat loaf, or what?
jokes.setup: Emotional moment about Krusty's painful memories
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious focus on food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely missing emotional weight in favor of practical concerns

jokes.text: Boy, you don't have to follow in my footsteps. Don't worry. I don't even like using the bathroom after you.
jokes.setup: Homer giving fatherly advice about not following in his footsteps
jokes.punchline: Bart's disgusted response about bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure with Bart's literal interpretation of 'following in footsteps'

jokes.text: A rabbi never exaggerates. A rabbi composes, he creates thoughts... he tells stories that may never have happened... but he does not exaggerate.
jokes.setup: Being accused of exaggerating about his son
jokes.punchline: Claims rabbis don't exaggerate while describing elaborate storytelling
jokes.explanation: Smart wordplay on semantic distinctions - good character-specific humor

jokes.text: A rabbi never exaggerates. A rabbi composes, he creates thoughts... he tells stories that may never have happened... but he does not exaggerate.
jokes.setup: Another rabbi says Krustofski is exaggerating
jokes.punchline: Krustofski's elaborate denial that itself demonstrates exaggeration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-refuting statement that proves the opposite of what it claims through its own verbose structure

jokes.text: If you were a musician or a jazz singer... this I could forgive, but this?
jokes.setup: Rabbi disowning his son for being a clown
jokes.punchline: Would forgive musician or jazz singer but not clown
jokes.explanation: Good hierarchy of acceptable professions showing even jazz singing ranks above clowning

jokes.text: Do you think about your father a lot? All the time. Except when I'm at the track.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if Krusty thinks about his father a lot
jokes.punchline: Says all the time except when gambling
jokes.explanation: Shows Krusty's gambling addiction and priorities with good economy

jokes.text: Milhouse, know any 'knock, knock' jokes?
jokes.setup: Uncomfortable silence after emotional story
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to lighten mood with kid humor
jokes.explanation: Good awkward kid response to heavy adult conversation

jokes.text: Hello? Hello?! Anybody there? I hear a phone ring... I'm listening, and there's no talking. Why would they call... if they don't want to talk?
jokes.setup: Rabbi answering phone with no response
jokes.punchline: Frustrated confusion about prank calls
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from old man confusion about modern annoyances

jokes.text: Didn't Itchy Junior look happy playing with his father? And didn't Scratchy Junior look happy playing with his dad... until they got run over by the thresher?
jokes.setup: Wholesome question about cartoon children playing with fathers
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of them being killed by farm equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark misdirection from wholesome to horrific, typical Itchy & Scratchy violence

jokes.text: For the love of God, cut to the commercial!
jokes.setup: Krusty watching the painful father-son cartoon
jokes.punchline: Desperate plea to escape the emotional trigger
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's emotional pain triggered by father-son content

jokes.text: I do a radio call-in show with him... every Sunday night. - Really? - I didn't know that. I mention it in my sermon every week.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy mentions his radio show with the rabbi
jokes.punchline: Points out he mentions it weekly but no one pays attention
jokes.explanation: Relatable humor about people not listening to sermons

jokes.text: Oh, that radio show. It's all the kids talk about on Monday at school.
jokes.setup: Learning about religious radio show
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic claim it's popular with kids
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm from Lisa about boring religious programming

jokes.text: Let me check my non-Christian Rolodex.
jokes.setup: Asked for rabbi's address
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy has organized contacts by religion
jokes.explanation: Funny detail about interfaith organization, very Lovejoy

jokes.text: I have no son! Great, we came all this way, and it's the wrong guy. I didn't mean that literally!
jokes.setup: Rabbi dramatically declares he has no son
jokes.punchline: Bart thinks they found the wrong person, rabbi clarifies
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Bart's literal interpretation of dramatic disowning language

jokes.text: There's all kinds of mishagoyim.
jokes.setup: Frustrated by prank callers hanging up
jokes.punchline: Uses Yiddish term for crazy people
jokes.explanation: Authentic character voice using appropriate Yiddish terminology

jokes.text: Yiddle, my man, you're a genius. I love my work.
jokes.setup: Lisa comes up with a plan
jokes.punchline: Bart uses Yiddish pet name for Lisa
jokes.explanation: Bart adopting Jewish terminology in context, mild character humor

jokes.text: Just get us another bowl of complimentary pickles.
jokes.setup: Waitress asking if they're ready to order
jokes.punchline: Kids just want more free pickles
jokes.explanation: Kid behavior of exploiting free food at restaurant

jokes.text: I want a nice sandwich, but the Joey Bishop-- too fatty. The Jackie Mason-- makes me gassy. The Bruce Willis? I don't like his work. What is this-- a Krusty the Clown? Ham, sausage and bacon, with a smidge of mayo. What? On white.
jokes.setup: Rabbi looking at sandwich menu named after celebrities
jokes.punchline: Krusty sandwich is pure treyf (non-kosher) foods
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating irony - rabbi's kosher son has sandwich with every forbidden meat, perfect character integration

jokes.text: You think you're funny? 50 million Frenchmen can't be wrong.
jokes.setup: Waiter asking about finding President Mitterrand
jokes.punchline: Rabbi's dismissive retort using old saying
jokes.explanation: Classic old-fashioned comeback using familiar saying

jokes.text: What's the one thing rabbis prize above everything else? Those stupid hats?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking what rabbis value most
jokes.punchline: Bart's shallow focus on their headwear
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Lisa's depth vs Bart's superficial observation

jokes.text: The Jews are a swinging bunch of people. I mean, I've heard of persecution... but what they went through is ridiculous. But the great thing is after thousands of years... of holding on and fighting... they finally made it.
jokes.setup: Bart quoting what sounds like profound religious text
jokes.punchline: It's actually from Sammy Davis Jr's autobiography
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection using sincere but informal language from unexpected source to convince rabbi

jokes.text: The Jews are a swinging bunch of people. I mean, I've heard of persecution... but what they went through is ridiculous. But the great thing is after thousands of years... of holding on and fighting... they finally made it.
jokes.setup: Bart quoting what sounds like profound religious wisdom
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's from Sammy Davis Jr.'s autobiography
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection using an unexpected source to make a serious point about entertainment and Jewish identity

jokes.text: The candy man? Maybe I'm completely upside down on this whole problem.
jokes.setup: Learning the quote was from Sammy Davis Jr
jokes.punchline: Recognizes him only as 'the candy man' from his hit song
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing rabbi's limited pop culture knowledge but willingness to reconsider

jokes.text: Hey, such a filthy habit. Who asked you?
jokes.setup: Rabbi commenting on Krusty smoking
jokes.punchline: Immediate father-son bickering despite reunion
jokes.explanation: Realistic family dynamic - even in reconciliation, old patterns emerge immediately

jokes.text: I'm not doing the spotlight bit.
jokes.setup: Krusty asking for spotlight for serious moment
jokes.punchline: Breaks character to complain about the production
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about TV production interrupting emotional moment

jokes.text: I got something in my eye. Take my hankie. Eeyugh!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming something in his eye during emotional scene
jokes.punchline: Marge's disgusted reaction to his handkerchief
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor with Homer's hygiene

jokes.text: Oy, a klop tsu deer! Hyuk! Hyuk! Hyuk!
jokes.setup: Emotional father-son reunion
jokes.punchline: Krusty mixes Yiddish expression with his trademark laugh
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration showing Krusty combining his heritage with his entertainer persona
